item.title: Conference‑Driven Development: Discovery Engines or Disappointing Disa... Brandt Keller & John Kjell
item.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
item.content:encoded:
Don't miss out! Join us at our next Flagship Conference: KubeCon + CloudNativeCon events in Amsterdam, The Netherlands (23-26 March, 2026). Connect with our current graduated, incubating, and sandbox projects as the community gathers to further the education and advancement of cloud native computing. Learn more at https://kubecon.io
Conference‑Driven Development: Discovery Engines or Disappointing Disasters? - Brandt Keller, Defense Unicorns & John Kjell, ControlPlane
As a project maintainer, when was the last time you saw a “can’t miss” session on a conference schedule? When was the last time you were inspired to wait after a talk to see if you could help take a speaker’s idea to the next level?
Conference‑Driven Development (CDD) proposes using CFP deadlines as structured R&D sprints. Could this pattern ethically accelerate open‑source innovation—or does it risk eroding reviewer trust?
This session investigates, rather than validates, CDD. We map a hypothetical workflow—submit → prototype → feedback → release → present—and test it against three lenses: ambition, transparency, and program integrity. Ethical guardrails (radical transparency, pre‑conf artifact pledge, fallback “lessons learned” narrative) frame the debate. Attendees will weigh potential gains in passion‑project velocity against dangers of over‑promising. They leave with a decision - not a mandate - to determine if, and how, their communities should adopt CDD.

item.title: Taming the Beast: The Move To Declarative API Validation in Kubernetes - Yongrui Lin, Google
item.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
item.content:encoded:
Don't miss out! Join us at our next Flagship Conference: KubeCon + CloudNativeCon events in Amsterdam, The Netherlands (23-26 March, 2026). Connect with our current graduated, incubating, and sandbox projects as the community gathers to further the education and advancement of cloud native computing. Learn more at https://kubecon.io
Taming the Beast: The Move To Declarative API Validation in Kubernetes - Yongrui Lin, Google
Handwritten API validation in Kubernetes is a 15,000-line beast, creating significant review toil and a high barrier to entry for new contributors. As maintainers, we spend countless hours navigating this complexity. This session introduces a project-wide effort to solve this by replacing manual Go code with simple, declarative tags in the API definitions.
This is more than a code generator; it's a fundamental shift in how we design and maintain APIs. We will share the architectural patterns behind our validation-gen tool, a safe migration strategy, and best practices your SIG can adopt. We'll also provide a look into our development process, detailing how we manage a long-lived feature branch while continuously syncing with upstream. You will learn how this effort simplifies contributions, reduces review overhead, and enables powerful features like validation ratcheting and cross-field reference validation.

item.title: Ask the Experts: Kubernetes Steering Committee
item.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
item.content:encoded:
Don't miss out! Join us at our next Flagship Conference: KubeCon + CloudNativeCon events in Amsterdam, The Netherlands (23-26 March, 2026). Connect with our current graduated, incubating, and sandbox projects as the community gathers to further the education and advancement of cloud native computing. Learn more at https://kubecon.io
Ask the Experts: Kubernetes Steering Committee
Get your questions answered live by representatives from the Kubernetes Steering Committee in this open-format session. Come prepared for an engaging and interactive discussion!

item.title: Tending the Kubernetes Dependency Tree: Bonsai or Bonfire? - Jordan Liggitt & Davanum Srinivas
item.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
item.content:encoded:
Don't miss out! Join us at our next Flagship Conference: KubeCon + CloudNativeCon events in Amsterdam, The Netherlands (23-26 March, 2026). Connect with our current graduated, incubating, and sandbox projects as the community gathers to further the education and advancement of cloud native computing. Learn more at https://kubecon.io
Tending the Kubernetes Dependency Tree: Bonsai or Bonfire? - Jordan Liggitt, Google & Davanum Srinivas, NVIDIA
With hundreds of dependencies adding over a million lines of code to Kubernetes, we have dealt with almost every possible issue that comes from a large dependency tree.
This session will show how we reduced the Kubernetes dependency graph by 30% in 1.34, and describe practices that can be applied to any project wrestling with dependency management.
* Reviewing dependency updates effectively * Isolating build/test/tool dependencies * Improving dependencies at their source * Reducing and removing problematic dependencies * Catching problematic upstream changes early * Tracking dependency health over time * Practical policies for consuming dependency security fixes

item.title: Shaping the Future of Developer Experienc... Daniel Oh, Kevin Dubois, Julien Semaan & Erica Hughberg
item.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
item.content:encoded:
Don't miss out! Join us at our next Flagship Conference: KubeCon + CloudNativeCon events in Amsterdam, The Netherlands (23-26 March, 2026). Connect with our current graduated, incubating, and sandbox projects as the community gathers to further the education and advancement of cloud native computing. Learn more at https://kubecon.io
Shaping the Future of Developer Experience: How TAG DevEx Supports CNCF Projects and Developers - Daniel Oh & Kevin Dubois, IBM; Julien Semaan, Akamai; Erica Hughberg, Tetrate.io
This panel explores how the CNCF's Developer Experience Technical Advisory Group (TAG) supports CNCF projects to improve the daily lives of app developers—and by extension, software quality and delivery. We will take you through our group’s goals and initiatives where the TAG collaborates with maintainers to identify and address friction points, streamline workflows, and foster best practices that boost app developer productivity across the cloud native ecosystem. While developer experience is central, TAG DevEx’s scope goes further, covering essential areas like databases, microservices, and API management, ensuring the broader infrastructure also supports a seamless development journey. Join us to discuss how to integrate developer experience considerations into project lifecycles better, ensuring maintainers receive the support needed to build projects that truly empower developers to build, deploy, and manage applications more efficiently within a Kubernetes environment.

item.title: Keynote: Meet the New Technic... Daniel Oh, Kashif Khan, Marina Moore, Rafael Brito & Rajas Kadodkar
item.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
item.content:encoded:
Don't miss out! Join us at our next Flagship Conference: KubeCon + CloudNativeCon events in Amsterdam, The Netherlands (23-26 March, 2026). Connect with our current graduated, incubating, and sandbox projects as the community gathers to further the education and advancement of cloud native computing. Learn more at https://kubecon.io
Keynote: Meet the New Technical Advisory Groups - Daniel Oh, Red Hat; Kashif Khan, metal3.io; Marina Moore, Edera; Rafael Brito, StormForge by CloudBolt; Rajas Kadodkar, Broadcom

item.title: TAG Workshop - Karena Angell, Mario Fahlandt, Brandt Keller, Dylan Page & Chad Beaudin
item.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
item.content:encoded:
Don't miss out! Join us at our next Flagship Conference: KubeCon + CloudNativeCon events in Amsterdam, The Netherlands (23-26 March, 2026). Connect with our current graduated, incubating, and sandbox projects as the community gathers to further the education and advancement of cloud native computing. Learn more at https://kubecon.io
TAG Workshop - Karena Angell, Red Hat; Mario Fahlandt, Kubermatic; Brandt Keller, Defense Unicorns; Dylan Page, Lambda.ai; Chad Beaudin, Boeing
This workshop continues the discussion on Technical Advisory Groups (TAGs) started in London. We’ll focus on the TAG/TCG distinction and socialize current initiatives. We will join breakout sessions to refine project involvement, the initiative lifecycle, and potential improvements. We look forward to everyone joining: be it TAG member or Project Member, we want your input!

item.title: Keynote: Opening & Welcome Remarks - Natali Vlatko, Mario Fahlandt & Karena Angell
item.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
item.content:encoded:
Don't miss out! Join us at our next Flagship Conference: KubeCon + CloudNativeCon events in Amsterdam, The Netherlands (23-26 March, 2026). Connect with our current graduated, incubating, and sandbox projects as the community gathers to further the education and advancement of cloud native computing. Learn more at https://kubecon.io
Keynote: Opening & Welcome Remarks - Natali Vlatko, Open Source Lead Architect, Cisco; Mario Fahlandt, Customer Delivery Architect, Kubermatic; Karena Angell, Chief of Staff, Global Field CTO, Red Hat & CNCF TOC Chair

item.title: Node Lifecycle WorkingGroup - Getting Started - Ryan Hallisey, NVIDIA & Filip Křepinský, Red Hat
item.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
item.content:encoded:
Don't miss out! Join us at our next Flagship Conference: KubeCon + CloudNativeCon events in Amsterdam, The Netherlands (23-26 March, 2026). Connect with our current graduated, incubating, and sandbox projects as the community gathers to further the education and advancement of cloud native computing. Learn more at https://kubecon.io
Node Lifecycle WorkingGroup - Getting Started - Ryan Hallisey, NVIDIA & Filip Křepinský, Red Hat
The Kubernetes ecosystem faces ongoing challenges in node maintenance and pod disruption, with multiple projects independently tackling similar problems. To address this fragmentation, the Node Lifecycle Working Group has formed to unify efforts and drive consensus on node lifecycle solutions.
This session will cover: - The scope and use cases of the Node Lifecycle Working Group - How the community collaborates to define, improve, and standardize node lifecycle management (from drain, health monitoring, Node maintenance, to Graceful Node Shutdown) and pod lifecycle management (from disruption, scheduling to autoscaling) - Key challenges and recent advancements - How you can get involved, contribute, and help shape the future of Node Lifecycle in Kubernetes

item.title: Navigating the Multi-Version Kubernetes Universe: How Emulation Version Shapes Your... Siyuan Zhang
item.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
item.content:encoded:
Don't miss out! Join us at our next Flagship Conference: KubeCon + CloudNativeCon events in Amsterdam, The Netherlands (23-26 March, 2026). Connect with our current graduated, incubating, and sandbox projects as the community gathers to further the education and advancement of cloud native computing. Learn more at https://kubecon.io
Navigating the Multi-Version Kubernetes Universe: How Emulation Version Shapes Your Contributions - Siyuan Zhang, Google
The Kubernetes project recently introduced the Compatibility Version (KEP-4330), a significant change aimed at making Kubernetes upgrades safer and more granular for cluster administrators. This new capability, enabling control plane components to emulate the behavior of prior Kubernetes releases, introduces exciting possibilities for stability but also brings important shifts to the developer workflow.
This talk will equip contributors with the essential knowledge to confidently navigate development in a multi-version Kubernetes environment, ensuring their contributions seamlessly integrate with the Emulation Version strategy. Join us to understand how this evolution makes Kubernetes more robust and how you can contribute to its continued stability.
This topic directly addresses a fundamental change in the Kubernetes development process that impacts every contributor. It's a crucial update for anyone actively involved in the kubernetes/kubernetes repository.

item.title: Workshop: On the Internet, Nobody Knows You’re a Bot - Justin Santa Barbara & Arnaud Meukam
item.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
item.content:encoded:
Don't miss out! Join us at our next Flagship Conference: KubeCon + CloudNativeCon events in Amsterdam, The Netherlands (23-26 March, 2026). Connect with our current graduated, incubating, and sandbox projects as the community gathers to further the education and advancement of cloud native computing. Learn more at https://kubecon.io
Workshop: On the Internet, Nobody Knows You’re a Bot - Justin Santa Barbara, Google & Arnaud Meukam, Independent
Justin and Arnaud will lead a discussion with the goal of establishing some shared principals for how the projects should work with AI, both how we feel about contributions with obvious AI usage and how we ourselves can leverage AI to lighten our maintainer load.
The CNCF community has always been about gathering wisdom from a large pool of contributors, of varying technical backgrounds, and sharing this expertise via our designs, our docs, our APIs and our code. AI’s share of these contributions is continuously increasing, ranging from writing better comments to fully automated code contributions.
We must decide how we want to work in this new reality. We are optimists that believe we can harness this technology to address many of our project challenges, such as maintainer burnout and how to nurture new (and existing) contributors, but we understand that many people have different views. We expect a vibrant debate!

item.title: Simplifying API Review - A Tool Based Approach for Kubernetes and Beyond - Bryce Palmer & Joel Speed
item.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
item.content:encoded:
Don't miss out! Join us at our next Flagship Conference: KubeCon + CloudNativeCon events in Amsterdam, The Netherlands (23-26 March, 2026). Connect with our current graduated, incubating, and sandbox projects as the community gathers to further the education and advancement of cloud native computing. Learn more at https://kubecon.io
Simplifying API Review - A Tool Based Approach for Kubernetes and Beyond - Bryce Palmer & Joel Speed, Red Hat
If you’ve ever written or maintained a Kubernetes native or custom resource based API, you’ve likely run into some challenges - especially around API best practices and review.
There is plenty of guidance available when working on Kubernetes, but what happens when you’re writing APIs for other projects?
Many projects define their own standards based on the existing guidance, but tailored for their project needs. Enforcement falls onto a small team, and the results can become inconsistent.
We’ve been working with sig-api-machinery and the current Kubernetes API reviewers to build tooling that encodes API best practices, to enable projects across the CNCF landscape to more consistently enforce API standards.
In this session, we’ll break down some of the things we look for during API reviews, why API review is important, and how tools like kube-api-linter and crdify help to simplify the process.
If you’re involved in developing or maintaining your projects APIs – this session is for you!

item.title: Unlocking Performance: Topology-Aware CPU Scheduling W... Praveen Krishna & Marlow Warnicke (Weston)
item.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
item.content:encoded:
Don't miss out! Join us at our next Flagship Conference: KubeCon + CloudNativeCon events in Amsterdam, The Netherlands (23-26 March, 2026). Connect with our current graduated, incubating, and sandbox projects as the community gathers to further the education and advancement of cloud native computing. Learn more at https://kubecon.io
Unlocking Performance: Topology-Aware CPU Scheduling With a DRA Driver - Praveen Krishna, Google & Marlow Warnicke (Weston), SchedMD LLC
You want optimal performance from your applications, especially in the areas of AI, Inference, Telco, and HPC. Without proper alignment for CPU, GPU, and NIC, this is not attainable. Unaligned CPUs slow down data transfers and Kubelet does not have a native way to coordinate CPU placement alongside other Dynamic Resource Allocation (DRA) resources, a critical bottleneck for high-performance workloads.
This session explores a CPU management solution using DRA. We'll show how a DRA driver can describe a node's CPU Topology and attributes (e.g., core types). This allows workloads to request CPUs CPUs guaranteed to align with other allocated devices. We will cover the high-level design of CPU allocation by the DRA driver and actuation by the node-level plugin. This approach offers powerful capabilities but includes challenges like performance overhead on the scheduler and the node. We aim to foster a discussion on the trade-offs, and the future evolution of CPU management in Kubernetes.

item.title: Keynote: Closing Remarks - Natali Vlatko, Mario Fahlandt & Karena Angell
item.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
item.content:encoded:
Don't miss out! Join us at our next Flagship Conference: KubeCon + CloudNativeCon events in Amsterdam, The Netherlands (23-26 March, 2026). Connect with our current graduated, incubating, and sandbox projects as the community gathers to further the education and advancement of cloud native computing. Learn more at https://kubecon.io
Keynote: Closing Remarks - Natali Vlatko, Open Source Lead Architect, Cisco; Mario Fahlandt, Customer Delivery Architect, Kubermatic; Karena Angell, Chief of Staff, Global Field CTO, Red Hat & CNCF TOC Chair

item.title: Behind the Proposals... Robert Pająk, Tyler Yahn, Liudmila Molkova, Trask Stalnaker & Josh Suereth
item.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
item.content:encoded:
Don't miss out! Join us at our next Flagship Conference: KubeCon + CloudNativeCon events in Amsterdam, The Netherlands (23-26 March, 2026). Connect with our current graduated, incubating, and sandbox projects as the community gathers to further the education and advancement of cloud native computing. Learn more at https://kubecon.io
Behind the Proposals: How OpenTelemetry Designs Take Shape - Robert Pająk & Tyler Yahn, Splunk; Liudmila Molkova & Trask Stalnaker, Microsoft; Josh Suereth, Google
Design proposals are at the heart of how open source projects evolve, and OpenTelemetry is no exception. This panel brings together contributors with a range of roles in the project, including governance and technical committee members, specification sponsors, and implementation maintainers. We will explore how OpenTelemetry handles design through OTEPs (OpenTelemetry Enhancement Proposals), semantic convention proposals, and new feature additions, both in the specification and implementations. The discussion will cover prototyping, design review, community engagement, and communicating changes. While focused on OpenTelemetry, many of the lessons and challenges such as building consensus, maintaining momentum, and balancing innovation with stability apply broadly to any open source project.

item.title: Did Google just kill OpenAI?
item.author: Fireship
item.content:encoded:
Get 20% off Mobbin Pro to make your apps not ugly - https://mobbin.com/fireship
Gemini 3 may have just put Google in prime position to have the best-performing AI model by the end of the year. But how powerful is it really?
#ai #coding #tech
💬 Chat with Me on Discord
https://discord.gg/fireship

- Atom One Dark - vscode-icons - Fira Code Font
🔖 Topics Covered - Gemini 3 release - Gemini 3 benchmarks - Google Antigravity

item.title: ChatLoopBackOff: Episode 74 (krkn )
item.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
item.content:encoded:
Join CNCF Ambassador Vyom Yadav as he explores krkn, a CNCF project focused on improving Kubernetes resilience through chaos and fault-injection workflows. In this live, first-time exploration, Vyom will take a look at how krkn helps teams understand system behavior under stress, validate reliability assumptions, and build confidence in their cloud native platforms.
Expect hands-on experimentation, real-time discoveries, and candid reflections as Vyom walks through the project’s features, community resources, and ecosystem integrations.
Whether you're interested in resilience testing, curious about emerging CNCF projects, or simply enjoy learning alongside the community, this episode offers an accessible introduction to how krkn approaches chaos engineering in Kubernetes environments.

item.title: ChatLoopBackOff: Episode 73 (Easegress)
item.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
item.content:encoded:
Join CNCF Ambassador Chad Crowell as he explores Easegress, a CNCF project built to simplify traffic orchestration in cloud native environments. In this live, first-time walkthrough, Chad will take a look at how Easegress handles service mesh traffic, API gateway capabilities, canary releases, and workflow automation—all through a lightweight and flexible architecture.
Expect real-time experimentation, open discussion, and plenty of “let’s see what happens” moments as Chad navigates the docs, tries out key features, and evaluates where Easegress fits within the broader CNCF landscape.
Whether you're curious about traffic management, interested in emerging CNCF Sandbox projects, or just enjoy learning alongside the community, this episode is the perfect way to get to know Easegress for the first time.

item.title: The Flipper Zero of Air Quality Monitors
item.author: Jeff Geerling
item.content:encoded:
It's a neat, portable Air Quality monitor, full of personality.
But is it worth $250?
Links to things I referenced in the video (some links are affiliate links):
- Air Lab: https://networkedartifacts.com/airlab/ - AirGradient One: https://www.airgradient.com/indoor/ - Temtop M2000C 2nd Generation: https://amzn.to/3XGbpbs - CO2 study referenced in the video: https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/23008272/
Support me on Patreon: https://www.patreon.com/geerlingguy Sponsor me on GitHub: https://github.com/sponsors/geerlingguy Merch: https://www.redshirtjeff.com 2nd Channel: https://www.youtube.com/@GeerlingEngineering 3rd Channel: https://www.youtube.com/@Level2Jeff
Contents:
00:00 - A $250 portable air quality monitor 01:14 - Versus AirGradient ONE 02:06 - Setting up an AirGradient with Home Assistant 05:36 - Setting up the Air Lab 07:24 - Air Lab Console walkthrough 11:28 - Stale air on a road trip 13:02 - Air quality at an indoor convention 13:40 - Battery life, Home Assistant integration 14:05 - DIY or Buy 15:18 - Bonus, the Temtop

item.title: 21+ Rust Pro Tips (TOP SECRET)
item.author: Let's Get Rusty
item.content:encoded:
👉Join the Rust Live Accelerator waitlist (30 seats): https://letsgetrusty.com/join .
Level up your Rust skills with practical insights and hard-earned lessons from mentoring over 100 Rust engineers. This video gives you clear, actionable techniques you can apply immediately to write cleaner, safer, and more efficient Rust code.
Chapter: 0:00 Intro 0:32 Tip 1 Use dbg! 0:47 Tip 2 Use todo! 1:03 Tip 3 Cargo Bacon 1:20 Tip 4 Cargo Nextest 1:35 Tip 5 Compiler-Driven Development 2:23 Tip 6 Type reuse 3:26 Tip 7 Use macros 4:02 Tip 8 Thin main.rs 4:20 Tip 9 Modules 4:34 Tip 10 Prelude 4:45 Tip 11 Control Visibility 5:10 Tip 12 Cargo workspace 5:56 Tip 13 Parse constructors 6:41 Tip 14 Type-state pattern 7:30 Tip 15 Clippy 7:45 Tip 16 must_use attribute 7:58 Tip 17 Clippy fix 8:16 Tip 18 rustfmt 8:40 Tip 19 rust toolchain 9:10 Tip 20 cargo audit 9:20 Tip 21 cargo deny 9:35 Tip 22 cargo tarpaulin 9:47 Tip 23 cargo chef

item.title: How to Get People Excited about Functional Programming • Russ Olsen & James Lewis
item.author: GOTO Conferences
item.content:encoded:
This interview was recorded at GOTO Copenhagen 2024. https://gotocph.com
Russ Olsen - Author of "Getting Clojure" & "Eloquent Ruby" James Lewis - Software Architect & Director at Thoughtworks
RESOURCES Russ https://bsky.app/profile/russolsen.bsky.social https://hachyderm.io/@russolsen https://github.com/russolsen https://www.linkedin.com/in/russolsen http://russolsen.com

Read the full abstract here: https://gotocph.com/2024/sessions/3522
RECOMMENDED BOOKS Russ Olsen • Getting Clojure • https://amzn.to/3J8zI8s Russ Olsen • Eloquent Ruby • https://amzn.to/37gOhcG Russ Olsen • Design Patterns in Ruby • https://amzn.to/3r2uBjW Barry O'Reilly • Unlearn • https://amzn.to/3O3DQeI Jez Humble, Joanne Molesky & Barry O'Reilly • Lean Enterprise • https://amzn.to/3Cpt6ET Sarah Wells • Enabling Microservice Success • https://amzn.to/4aa8xrv Martin Fowler • Refactoring • https://amzn.to/3EVcHXQ Matthew Skelton & Manuel Pais • Team Topologies • http://amzn.to/3sVLyLQ Inspiring Tech Leaders - The Technology Podcast (https://www.priceroberts.com/) Interviews with Tech Leaders and insights on the latest emerging technology trends.
Listen on: Apple Podcasts (https://podcasts.apple.com/podcast/id1563026924) Spotify (https://open.spotify.com/show/5ihWZxxIa2rEnzbx5tdKfH)

item.title: ChatLoopBackOff: Episode 72 (Kagent)
item.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
item.content:encoded:
In this episode, CNCF Ambassador Satyam Soni explores Kagent, an open source observability agent designed to simplify how developers collect, process, and export telemetry data from Kubernetes environments. Join Satyam as he takes a first-time look at how Kagent brings flexibility and visibility to cloud native systems—bridging the gap between infrastructure metrics and actionable insights.
From setup to data flow, Satyam will look into what makes Kagent stand out in the observability space, how it integrates with existing CNCF tools, and what developers should know when evaluating it for production use. Expect plenty of experimentation, learning, and candid reflections as Satyam unpacks this innovative project in real time.

item.title: Modern X86 Assembly Language Programming • Daniel Kusswurm & Matt Godbolt • GOTO 2025
item.author: GOTO Conferences
item.content:encoded:
This interview was recorded for the GOTO Book Club. #GOTOcon #GOTObookclub http://gotopia.tech/bookclub
Check out more here: https://gotopia.tech/episodes/411
Daniel Kusswurm - SW Developer, Computer Scientist & Author of "Modern X86 Assembly Language Programming" & many more books Matt Godbolt - Low-level Latency Geek & Creator of Compiler Explorer @MattGodbolt
RESOURCES Matt https://bsky.app/profile/matt.godbolt.org https://xania.org https://github.com/mattgodbolt https://www.linkedin.com/in/godbolt https://twitter.com/mattgodbolt https://godbolt.org
Links https://link.springer.com https://youtu.be/hgcNM-6wr34 https://youtu.be/nI15yE2yIoI https://youtu.be/ehuYrQvFcFg https://youtu.be/lg0RYLlmjJM https://youtu.be/7PFwUpXKLrg https://youtu.be/_XbOTRunqHk https://youtu.be/-HNpim5x-IE https://youtu.be/EFXxXFHpS0M https://youtu.be/MkbgZMCTUyU https://youtu.be/y30jmDB7QLc https://youtu.be/qmh7Uppd3x0 https://youtu.be/fDGWWpHlzvw https://youtu.be/VwPCtxxvwjI https://youtu.be/n9aSa9XVuiQ
DESCRIPTION Matt Godbolt, creator of Compiler Explorer, interviews Daniel Kusswurm, author of "Modern X86 Assembly Language Programming", about the enduring relevance of assembly language in modern software development. They explore what SIMD (Single Instruction, Multiple Data) is and why it enables dramatic performance improvements for specific computational tasks, discuss when assembly language programming is justified versus using compiler intrinsics, and examine the challenges of writing vectorized algorithms. Dan shares insights from his career optimizing image analysis software and explains how understanding low-level architecture can improve code quality even when writing in higher-level languages.
TIMECODES 00:00 Intro 01:39 What is Assembly Language & why does it matter? 05:58 Understanding SIMD programming 10:11 Writing SIMD algorithms 13:16 Challenges of SIMD programming 18:58 Intrinsics vs Assembly Language 22:41 Real-world optimization examples 24:42 Development workflow & benchmarking 30:12 Outro
RECOMMENDED BOOKS Daniel Kusswurm • Modern X86 Assembly Language Programming v3 • https://amzn.to/44hIVZf Daniel Kusswurm • Modern X86 Assembly Language Programming v2 • https://amzn.to/3LJlMIZ Daniel Kusswurm • Modern X86 Assembly Language Programming v1 • https://amzn.to/43DPANj Daniel Kusswurm • Modern Arm Assembly Language Programming • https://amzn.to/4rbyH6M Daniel Kusswurm • Practical C++ STL Programming • https://amzn.to/4ickAdq Daniel Kusswurm • Modern Parallel Programming with C++ and Assembly Language • https://amzn.to/4o5J3SF
https://bsky.app/profile/gotocon.com https://twitter.com/GOTOcon https://www.linkedin.com/company/goto- https://www.instagram.com/goto_con https://www.facebook.com/GOTOConferences #X86 #Assembly #AssemblyLanguage #SIMD #SIMDAlgorithms #SSE #Compiler #Intrinsics #Benchmarking #CompilerExplorer #ARM #ARMx64 #64bit #AVX #AVX2 #AVX512 #TodayInTech #SoftwareDevelopment #SoftwareEngineering #Programming #DanielKusswurm #DanKusswurm #MattGodbolt #BookClub
CHANNEL MEMBERSHIP BONUS Join this channel to get early access to videos & other perks: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCs_tLP3AiwYKwdUHpltJPuA/join
Looking for a unique learning experience? Attend the next GOTO conference near you! Get your ticket at https://gotopia.tech Sign up for updates and specials at https://gotopia.tech/newsletter
SUBSCRIBE TO OUR CHANNEL - new videos posted almost daily. https://www.youtube.com/user/GotoConferences/?sub_confirmation=1

item.title: CNCF On-Demand: Providing Replicated Kubernetes Storage with OpenEBS
item.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
item.content:encoded: OpenEBS is a container-native storage solution that provides replicated persistent volumes for Kubernetes workloads, using technologies like NVMe and the Mayastor engine to synchronously replicate data across multiple nodes for high availability. This video demonstrates how OpenEBS deploys storage engines as microservices within Kubernetes pods, in order to turn any available storage on worker nodes into replicated persistent volumes. A key point is that OpenEBS's replicated storage maintains complete copies of data on each node rather than sharding data across nodes like traditional distributed storage, which reduces the blast radius during failures.

item.title: CNCF On-Demand: Simplifying Cluster And Application Lifecycle (CAPI, CAREN and Gitops)
item.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
item.content:encoded:
Managing the lifecycle of Kubernetes clusters and applications at scale often leads to operational complexity, fragmented tooling, and inconsistent environments. This session explores a unified, declarative approach to simplify and automate these processes using Cluster API (CAPI), ClusterClass, CAREN, and GitOps with Flux.
Starting with how CAPI standardizes cluster provisioning and upgrades across cloud providers, while ClusterClass brings reusable, templatized cluster blueprints for consistent configurations. CAREN (Cluster API Runtime Environment) further streamlines day-2 operations by orchestrating lifecycle events through composable runtime hooks. Combined with Flux GitOps, the entire cluster and application stack becomes self-managing and continuously reconciled from source control.

item.title: Providing Replicated Kubernetes Storage with OpenEBS
item.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]

item.title: The entire internet just crashed... again
item.author: Fireship
item.content:encoded:
Break prod less with Sentry’s AI Code Review - https://sentry.io/fireship
This week, it was Cloudflare's turn to take down your favourite domain. In today's video, we'll try to find out why the internet keeps falling apart...
#programming #coding #cloudflare

item.title: Beyond Chat: Bringing Models to the Canvas • Lu Wilson • GOTO 2025
item.author: GOTO Conferences
item.content:encoded:
This presentation was recorded at GOTO Copenhagen 2025. #GOTOcon #GOTOcph https://gotocph.com
Lu Wilson - Software Engineer at tldraw @TodePond
RESOURCES https://bsky.app/profile/todepond.com https://mastodon.social/@TodePond https://twitter.com/TodePond https://www.todepond.com
ABSTRACT Whenever a new technology appears, our first instinct as developers is to offer "text" as the primary method of interaction. This has happened throughout computing history, with the computer terminal, with early smartphones, and now it's happening again with AI.
At tldraw, we’ve been working on moving AI interaction away from the chat-based interface, towards a richer canvas environment. It hasn't been easy! I'll show you all the challenges we've faced, and how we're currently overcoming them. Some of the solutions have been surprising. [...]
TIMECODES 00:00 Intro
Read the full abstract here: https://gotocph.com/2025/sessions/3773
RECOMMENDED BOOKS Phil Winder • Reinforcement Learning • https://amzn.to/3t1S1VZ Alex Castrounis • AI for People and Business • https://amzn.to/3NYKKTo Holden Karau, Trevor Grant, Boris Lublinsky, Richard Liu & Ilan Filonenko • Kubeflow for Machine Learning • https://amzn.to/3JVngcx Kelleher & Tierney • Data Science (The MIT Press Essential Knowledge series) • https://amzn.to/3AQmIRg Lakshmanan, Robinson & Munn • Machine Learning Design Patterns • https://amzn.to/2ZD7t0x Lakshmanan, Görner & Gillard • Practical Machine Learning for Computer Vision • https://amzn.to/3m9HNjP
https://bsky.app/profile/gotocon.com https://twitter.com/GOTOcon https://www.linkedin.com/company/goto- https://www.instagram.com/goto_con https://www.facebook.com/GOTOConferences #AI #GenAI #GenerativeAI #ArtificialIntelligence #ChatGPT #ML #MakeRealtldraw #MakeReal #tldraw #Teachtldraw #TodayInTech #AIDriven #LuWilson

item.title: CNL: Kyverno 1.16 — Advancing Policy as Code with CEL and SDK
item.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
item.content:encoded: Kyverno 1.16 delivers major advancements in policy-as-code for Kubernetes, centered on a new generation of CEL-based policies now available in beta, with a clear path to GA. This release introduces partial support for namespaced CEL policies to confine enforcement and minimize RBAC, aligning with least-privilege best practices. Observability is significantly enhanced with full metrics for CEL policies and native event generation, enabling precise visibility and faster troubleshooting. Security and governance get sharper controls through fine-grained policy exceptions tailored for CEL policies, and validation use cases broaden with the integration of an HTTP authorizer into ValidatingPolicy. Finally, we’re debuting the Kyverno SDK, laying the foundation for ecosystem integrations and custom tooling, with maturity planned in 1.17. Join this session to learn what’s new, how to adopt CEL policies confidently, and what’s coming next.

item.title: Beyond the Hype: Real Talk on AI-Assisted Development • Jessica Kerr & Diana Montalion
item.author: GOTO Conferences
item.content:encoded:
This interview was recorded for GOTO Unscripted. https://gotopia.tech
Jessica Kerr - Engineering Manager of Developer Relation at Honeycomb.io & Symmathecist Diana Montalion - Systems Architect, Mentrix Founder & Author of "Learning Systems Thinking"
RESOURCES Jessica https://bsky.app/profile/jessitron.bsky.social (https://github.com/jessitron) https://linkedin.com/in/jessicakerr https://www.twitch.tv/jessitronica (https://www.instagram.com/jessitronica) https://jessitron.com
Diana https://bsky.app/profile/dianamontalion.com (https://hachyderm.io/@diana) https://www.linkedin.com/in/dianamontalion (https://github.com/dianamontalion) https://montalion.com https://learningsystemsthinking.com
DESCRIPTION Systems architect Diana Montalion and engineering manager Jessica Kerr cut through the AI coding hype to explore what these tools actually do well - and where they have room for improvement. Moving beyond the "AI will replace developers" narrative, they reveal how AI assistants excel at the tedious work of typing, scaffolding, and error handling while remaining surprisingly bad at the nuanced thinking that experienced developers bring to complex systems.
Their discussion illuminates a more mature relationship with AI tools: one where developers maintain agency over design decisions while leveraging AI's strengths in automation, synthesis, and rapid prototyping. The result is a pragmatic roadmap for using AI to amplify human expertise rather than replace it.
RECOMMENDED BOOKS Diana Montalion • Learning Systems Thinking • https://amzn.to/3ZpycdJ Andrew Harmel-Law • Facilitating Software Architecture • https://amzn.eu/d/5kZKVfU Donella H. Meadows • Thinking in Systems • https://amzn.to/3XtqYCV Yu-kai Chou • Actionable Gamification • https://amzn.to/45D8bHA Inspiring Tech Leaders - The Technology Podcast (https://www.priceroberts.com/) Interviews with Tech Leaders and insights on the latest emerging technology trends.
Listen on: Apple Podcasts (https://podcasts.apple.com/podcast/id1563026924) Spotify (https://open.spotify.com/show/5ihWZxxIa2rEnzbx5tdKfH)
Bluesky (https://bsky.app/profile/gotocon.com) Twitter (https://twitter.com/GOTOcon) Instagram (https://www.instagram.com/goto_con) LinkedIn (https://www.linkedin.com/company/goto-) Facebook (https://www.facebook.com/GOTOConferences)
CHANNEL MEMBERSHIP BONUS Join this channel to get early access to videos & other perks: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCs_tLP3AiwYKwdUHpltJPuA/join

item.title: Sponsored Keynote: From Policy to Production: Flux and GitOps as Governance Control... Andrew Martin
item.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
item.content:encoded:
Don't miss out! Join us at our next Flagship Conference: KubeCon + CloudNativeCon events in Amsterdam, The Netherlands (23-26 March, 2026). Connect with our current graduated, incubating, and sandbox projects as the community gathers to further the education and advancement of cloud native computing. Learn more at https://kubecon.io
Sponsored Keynote: From Policy to Production: Flux and GitOps as Governance Control Tower for Secure Software Delivery - Andrew Martin, ControlPlane
At the dawn of the first ever FluxCon, Andy will share his perspective on Flux’s strong community, broad adoption, both expert-led and “DIY,” and a clear intent to make GitOps the foundation for secure, compliant, and scalable infrastructure and software delivery. In the opening of the keynote, he’ll deliver key project updates, highlighting recent achievements and offering a glimpse of what’s on the roadmap.
Flux’s adoption is evolving from a deployment engine into a governance control tower for software delivery, ensuring compliance, security, and trust across environments. Andy will bring his perspective on how teams in regulated industries, including major banks, use Flux to embed policy and auditability directly into their workflows, shifting from manual operations to continuous governance by design.
Andy will also introduce the vision behind Flux’s MCP, laying the foundation for AI-assisted GitOps. This will bring even greater clarity and control to complex systems and configurations. As part of this, Andy will reference the AI reference architecture and plans to evolve Flux MCP.
Finally, there will be a moment to celebrate the growing ecosystem, from core maintainers to contributors at GitLab, Microsoft, and projects like Headlamp, to many others helping drive Flux forward. To conclude, Andy will highlight key contributions from the community and ecosystem, including end-user stories such as RBC’s, and thank the attendees and the CNCF for their support of the project

item.title: Day 3 Highlight: KubeCon + CloudNativeCon Atlanta 2025
item.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
item.content:encoded:
Day three of KubeCon + CloudNativeCon North America came too fast! What a week it was learning about Kubernetes AI Conformance, Platform Engineering, and Security.
We have so much gratitude for our generous members, sponsors, contributors, and Gold Standard event staff. And a huge thanks to you, who attended. See you at the next one!

item.title: From Apache Spark to Fighting Health Insurance Denials • Holden Karau & Julian Wood • GOTO 2025
item.author: GOTO Conferences
item.content:encoded:
This interview was recorded for GOTO Unscripted. #GOTOcon #GOTOunscripted https://gotopia.tech
Check out more here: https://gotopia.tech/articles/410
Holden Karau - Co-Founder at Fight Health Insurance @HoldenKarau Julian Wood - Serverless Developer Advocate at AWS @julian_wood
RESOURCES Holden https://bsky.app/profile/holdenkarau.com https://twitter.com/holdenkarau https://github.com/holdenk https://www.linkedin.com/in/holdenkarau http://holdenkarau.com
Julian https://bsky.app/profile/julianwood.com https://twitter.com/julian_wood https://github.com/julianwood http://www.wooditwork.com https://www.linkedin.com/in/julianrwood
Links https://fighthealthinsurance.com https://youtu.be/4xsBQYdHgn8 https://youtu.be/GZfJM7lhi8o https://youtu.be/7M7wt3nY0dg https://youtu.be/L2kkRvqHcFg https://youtu.be/dsiTp4sCHaE https://youtu.be/9gZMnJ2XPkM https://youtu.be/dqDLDngR-og https://youtu.be/Q5muBDDdhJg https://youtu.be/w3zkqfTN1fc https://youtu.be/wiU3QIcf4fU https://youtu.be/dUZ5TrSReZc https://youtu.be/IX3300umebg https://youtu.be/mUTx61t443A https://youtu.be/MxlLDSUBJX8 https://youtu.be/QgrChOltYek https://youtu.be/8KntBPOdfWA
DESCRIPTION Apache Spark contributor Holden Karau discusses the evolution of distributed data processing, the challenges of integrating machine learning with big data tools, and the technical complexities of working with GPUs at scale.
The conversation takes a personal turn as Holden reveals her latest project: an open-source AI tool that helps people appeal health insurance claim denials. Drawing from personal experiences with denied claims, she has built a system that trains on independent medical review data to generate effective appeals, making it freely available at https://fighthealthinsurance.com while grappling with the classic open-source challenge of sustainable funding.
TIMECODES 00:00 Intro 00:26 Apache Spark: A decade of distributed data processing 07:03 GPU integration & resource management 09:51 Common mistakes & best practices in data processing 14:59 Building an AI tool to fight insurance denials 18:36 How the insurance appeal tool works 26:17 Technical challenges & open source sustainability 39:55 Outro
RECOMMENDED BOOKS Holden Karau • Distributed Computing 4 Kids • https://www.distributedcomputing4kids.com Holden Karau • Scaling Python with Dask • https://www.oreilly.com/library/view/scaling-python-with/9781098119867 Holden Karau & Boris Lublinsky • Scaling Python with Ray • https://amzn.to/44GU6cC Holden Karau & Rachel Warren • High Performance Spark • https://amzn.to/3v2eLbn Holden Karau, Konwinski, Wendell & Zaharia • Learning Spark • https://amzn.to/397e2NE Holden Karau & Krishna Sankar • Fast Data Processing with Spark 2nd Edition • https://amzn.to/3xKhXKu Holden Karau • Fast Data Processing with Spark 1st Edition • https://amzn.to/3rHQgOu
https://bsky.app/profile/gotocon.com https://twitter.com/GOTOcon https://www.linkedin.com/company/goto- https://www.instagram.com/goto_con https://www.facebook.com/GOTOConferences #ApacheSpark #OpenSource #DistributedData #DataProcessing #LLMs #GenAI #HealthInsurance #HealthInsuranceAppeals #FightHealthInsurance #InsuranceDenials #GPU #Programming #TodayInTech #HoldenKarau #JulianWood
CHANNEL MEMBERSHIP BONUS Join this channel to get early access to videos & other perks: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCs_tLP3AiwYKwdUHpltJPuA/join
Looking for a unique learning experience? Attend the next GOTO conference near you! Get your ticket at https://gotopia.tech Sign up for updates and specials at https://gotopia.tech/newsletter
